title: G4: Full nonlinear error propagation to sigma(v_perp), with cross-covariances

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap.** The map from observables $(\alpha,\delta,D,v_{\rm los},\mu_{\alpha*},\mu_\delta;\,\text{M31 params})$ to $v_{\perp}=\mathbf v\cdot\hat n$ is NONLINEAR: it contains trigonometric sky-coordinate factors, the product distance$\times$PM (transverse velocity $=4.74\,\mu[\text{mas/yr}]\,D[\text{kpc}]$ km/s), the M31 frame subtraction (G2), and the plane normal (G3).

**What to do.** (i) Monte Carlo: draw $\ge 10^4$ samples of ALL inputs from their joint covariance (including M31 systemic PM from G2 and $\hat n$ from G3); recompute $v_{\perp,i}$ each draw; read off $\sigma_i$ AND the full cross-satellite covariance $\Sigma_{v_\perp}$. (ii) Linearized (Jacobian) propagation as an independent cross-check. (iii) Identify the dominant uncertainty term (typically PM error $\times$ distance, $\approx 37$ km/s per mas/century at 780 kpc).

**Requirement.** A diagonal/independent error treatment is explicitly WRONG here because of G2 and G3; the off-diagonal structure must be retained.
